skill.name: k8s-manifest-generator
skill.description: Create production-ready Kubernetes manifests for Deployments, Services, ConfigMaps, and Secrets following best practices and security standards.
skill.summary: Generate secure, production-ready Kubernetes YAML manifests with built-in best practices for security, resource management, and high availability.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 285 pattern matches across 7 files, but all findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This skill contains only YAML templates and Markdown documentation - no executable code. Shell scripts in ConfigMap templates are meant to be stored in Kubernetes, not executed by the skill. URLs and IPs are placeholder examples. No actual security risks exist.

content.value_statement: Creating secure Kubernetes manifests manually is error-prone and time-consuming. This skill provides templates and best practices for Deployments, Services, ConfigMaps, and Secrets that follow cloud-native standards.
actual_capabilities: Generate Deployment manifests with resource limits, health probes, and security contexts
Create Service definitions for ClusterIP, LoadBalancer, NodePort, and Headless services
Build ConfigMap templates for environment variables and configuration files
Design Secret resource structures for sensitive data management
Apply Kubernetes labeling conventions and annotation standards
Validate manifests against Kubernetes API specifications
limitations: Generates YAML templates only - does not deploy to actual clusters
Requires user to provide application-specific values like image names and ports
Does not support advanced features like Operators or Custom Resource Definitions
Templates follow general best practices but may need customization for specific environments

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Deploying Microservices
use_cases.description: Generate consistent, secure manifests for multiple microservices with proper resource limits, health checks, and security contexts following organizational standards.

use_cases.title: Developer Containerizing Applications
use_cases.description: Create initial Kubernetes manifests when migrating from Docker Compose or running applications locally, with guidance on probes, resources, and configuration.

use_cases.title: Platform Team Building Reference Templates
use_cases.description: Generate standardized templates for internal developer platforms with pre-configured security policies, monitoring annotations, and logging configurations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Deployment Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment manifest for a Node.js API running on port 3000 with the image myregistry/api:v1.2.0. Include basic resource limits and a health check endpoint at /health.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Application Stack
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete Kubernetes manifest set for a Python web application including: Deployment with 3 replicas, ClusterIP Service, ConfigMap for environment variables (DATABASE_URL, LOG_LEVEL, CACHE_TTL), and Secret for database credentials. Include liveness and readiness probes.

prompt_templates.title: Production-Hardened Manifest
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production-ready Kubernetes Deployment with: pod anti-affinity for high availability, pod disruption budget, network policy recommendations, security context running as non-root with dropped capabilities, resource quotas, and Prometheus monitoring annotations. Application is a Java Spring Boot service.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Environment Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate ConfigMap and Deployment manifests that support multiple environments (dev, staging, prod) with environment-specific values for replica count, resource limits, log levels, and feature flags. Use Kubernetes best practices for configuration separation.

output_examples.input: Create a basic Deployment for an nginx web server
output_examples.output: A YAML manifest with Deployment resource including: metadata with labels, spec with 3 replicas, container definition with nginx:1.25 image, port 80 exposed, resource requests/limits (256Mi/512Mi memory, 250m/500m CPU), and HTTP health probes on port 80.

output_examples.input: Generate a ConfigMap for application settings
output_examples.output: A YAML manifest with ConfigMap resource containing: metadata with app labels, data section with APP_ENV=production, LOG_LEVEL=info, DATABASE_HOST, DATABASE_PORT, and an embedded application.yaml configuration file with server, logging, and database settings.

best_practices: Always set resource requests and limits to prevent resource starvation and ensure predictable scheduling
Implement liveness, readiness, and startup probes to enable Kubernetes health management
Use specific image tags instead of latest to ensure reproducible deployments
anti_patterns: Using :latest image tags which causes unpredictable deployments and rollback issues
Running containers as root without security context, increasing privilege escalation risks
Hardcoding secrets directly in manifests instead of using Kubernetes Secret resources

faq.question: What Kubernetes resources can this skill generate?
faq.answer: This skill generates manifests for Deployments, Services (ClusterIP, LoadBalancer, NodePort, Headless), ConfigMaps, Secrets, and PersistentVolumeClaims. It focuses on core resources commonly used for application deployments.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy manifests to my cluster?
faq.answer: No, this skill only generates YAML manifest files. You must apply them to your cluster using kubectl apply or a GitOps tool like ArgoCD or Flux.

faq.question: Are the generated manifests production-ready?
faq.answer: Yes, templates follow Kubernetes best practices including resource limits, health probes, security contexts, and proper labeling. However, always review and test in a non-production environment first.

faq.question: Can I customize the templates for my organization?
faq.answer: Absolutely. The templates use placeholder values that you should replace. You can modify any field to match your organization's naming conventions, security policies, and infrastructure requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill support Helm charts or Kustomize?
faq.answer: This skill generates raw YAML manifests. For Helm or Kustomize templating, use those tools separately or request manifest structures compatible with your templating approach.

faq.question: What security features are included in the manifests?
faq.answer: Templates include security contexts for non-root users, dropped capabilities, read-only filesystems, and recommendations for Secret management. They follow Pod Security Standards baseline profile.
